mnemonics.title: SDN vs. NFV Focus
mnemonics.content: SDN: Separates Control & Data. NFV: Functions are Virtual. Remember 'S' for Separation (SDN) and 'V' for Virtualization (NFV).

mnemonics.title: SDN Layer Order
mnemonics.content: Always Control From the Top: Application (Top) -> Control (Middle) -> Infrastructure (Bottom). Acts like a company: Apps are management, Control is middle-manager, Infrastructure is workers.

mnemonics.title: SDN APIs
mnemonics.content: North goes UP, South goes DOWN. Northbound API connects apps to controller (upward request). Southbound API (e.g., OpenFlow) connects controller to switches (downward commands).

mnemonics.title: NFV Benefits - CASE
mnemonics.content: CASE: Cost reduction (COTS servers), Agility (rapid deployment), Scalability (elastic VNFs), Edge deployment (place VNFs anywhere).
